title: Abhishek breaks KL Rahul's record in England, becomes fastest ever to...
description: India's first T20I against England was abandoned due to rain after the visitors posted 189/7. Abhishek Sharma starred with a record-breaking 59 off 24 balls, registering the fastest T20I fifty by an Indian in England, while captain Shreyas Iyer scored 68. Shivam Dube's unbeaten 42 provided a late flourish before persistent rain forced a no-result.

title: India-Japan trade: PM Modi, 'younger sister' Takaichi sign MoC
description: India and Japan elevated their trade and strategic ties with a new Memorandum of Cooperation signed during the 16th Annual Summit in New Delhi. Prime Minister Modi welcomed his Japanese counterpart, highlighting shared priorities for a free and prosperous Indo-Pacific. The leaders discussed initiatives to boost Japanese investment in India, aiming for significant growth in capital and company presence, underscoring a strengthened partnership built on trust and shared values.

description: The important thing to understand is that ITR filing is a statutory requirement and an important financial record-keeping. For the financial year 2025-26, the basic exemption limit for individuals below 60 years of age is Rs 2.5 lakh under the old income tax regime and Rs 4 lakh under the new income tax regime.

title: Tattoo on right forearm costs two CRPF inspectors promotion; Delhi HC dismisses pleas
description: Two CRPF inspectors were denied promotion to Assistant Commandant due to tattoos on their right forearms, deemed the saluting limb. The Delhi High Court upheld the decision, clarifying that while tattoos aren't banned, strict rules govern their placement, size, and content. The court ruled that having tattoos removed after being declared unfit doesn't qualify them for the current vacancies, advising them to reapply for future examinations.

title: Thailand: 11-year-old crashes truck into group of pilgrims; 8 monks killed
description: Tragedy struck in northeastern Thailand as at least eight monks on a pilgrimage were killed when an 11-year-old boy lost control of a pickup truck. The vehicle veered off the road, striking the group. Five monks died at the scene, with three more succumbing to their injuries at a hospital. Fourteen others were hospitalized following the devastating incident. Authorities are investigating the cause.

title: Balogun joins Zidane, Ronaldinho in unwanted World Cup history after red card vs Bosnia
description: USA secured a spot in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina. However, striker Folarin Balogun made unwanted history, becoming the fourth player to score and get sent off in a knockout match. Despite his red card for an accidental foul, the US team held on for the victory, but Balogun's suspension creates a selection headache for their next game against Belgium.

description: Goa's cabinet has approved the 'Mhajo Flat' initiative, providing essential support for countless flat owners embroiled in longstanding ownership disputes. This program empowers residents to establish cooperative societies, enabling them to secure legal ownership of their homes, even amid builder absence or landowner conflicts. With a minimal fee and stamp duty, this move facilitates rightful ownership and encourages the revitalization of aging properties.

description: A Jharkhand court has ordered the reinstatement of a peon sacked for taking office tea and biscuits home. The court deemed the dismissal "grossly disproportionate," highlighting a vague show-cause notice and the lack of consideration for his plea. The bench emphasized that such a harsh penalty for a low-paid employee was insensitive, directing his re-employment with partial back wages.

The vessel, carrying valuable crude oil, endured shrapnel damage to its bridge during hostilities in the Middle East. Despite facing prolonged delays and navigating a reopened Strait of Hormuz, the ship and its entire crew arrived safely, showcasing remarkable resilience and professionalism.

The move comes amid increasing government scrutiny of AI's impact on jobs and cybersecurity.

Despite this, Tewari acknowledged the party's long-standing support and his own dedication. The appointments, announced by the AICC, saw other prominent leaders like Charanjit Singh Channi and Vijay Inder Singla given key positions for the upcoming 2027 Assembly elections.

description: Disturbing videos alleging child abuse at a Bengaluru daycare center have led to the arrest of five nannies. The facility, located on Capgemini's campus, reportedly subjected toddlers to mistreatment, including being placed in a washing machine and locked in toilets. An official who previously reported the abuse was allegedly fired. Police are investigating the videos and other evidence, vowing to hold all involved accountable.

Monsoon arrives in India: Heavy rain lashes Delhi, Mumbai; to spread to northern, western states
description: Delhi finally welcomed the southwest monsoon on Thursday, five days past its usual arrival, bringing much-needed relief from heat. The monsoon is now widespread across northwest India, with heavy rains expected in Maharashtra, Goa, and Gujarat. This marks the first July onset for Delhi since 2021, as the monsoon rapidly advances, nearing full country coverage ahead of schedule.

title: Cisco to roll out AI agents to entire workforce, says in-house AI is most efficient
description: Cisco is rolling out personalized AI agents to all 90,000 employees starting August, a significant corporate AI adoption. These agents will manage tasks and optimize model selection for efficiency and cost. The company is also investing in upskilling and sees AI as a major growth opportunity, with AI-related orders surging. Despite the potential, Cisco acknowledges the adoption process can be challenging.

Watch: Congo coach stunned after learning of father's death during press conference
description: Congo DR's World Cup dream ended in heartbreak as England secured a 2-1 victory. Minutes after the match, coach Sébastien Desabre received devastating news of his father's passing during a post-match press conference. The emotional announcement stunned reporters and brought the media interaction to an abrupt halt, overshadowing the team's historic tournament run.

title: US de-lists four Indian companies sanctioned for aiding Russia's military base
description: In a significant move, the US Treasury has lifted sanctions on four Indian companies that were previously accused of supplying advanced technologies to the Russian military. The firms include RRG Engineering Technologies and Lokesh Machines from Hyderabad, Galaxy Bearings from Ahmedabad, and Shaurya Aeronautics from New Delhi. However, the US department has not provided specific reasons for their delisting.

title: TGBIE extends IPE first year phase II admission till July 31
Buying a home? Here's how women can save on stamp duty across states
description: In multiple Indian states, financial incentives aimed at women buying property, such as slashed stamp duties and preferential home loan rates, are making a significant impact. Enhanced by government housing efforts like PMAY, these programs not only enhance women's property ownership but also promote financial equality and inclusivity, paving the way for joint ownership and facilitating access to higher mortgage amounts to create a balanced real estate market.

title: New EPF scheme : What it means for your provident fund savings
description: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

title: 7 killed, 6 injured as boulders cave in at quarry near Bengaluru
description: A devastating rockfall at a quarry in Ramanagara claimed the lives of seven workers and injured at least six others. The tragedy occurred at Cauvery Crushers when loose boulders collapsed on a group of around 20 men. Families of the deceased, who were migrant workers seeking daily wages, are left heartbroken. Authorities have pledged action against illegal quarrying activities.

title: Ranveer Singh shares a message as Dhurandhar releases in Japan, eyes RRR’s Rs 139 cr record

Watch: Yashasvi Jaiswal flaunts 'Messi' jersey during France vs Sweden WC match
description: Dreams do come true! Cricket star Yashasvi Jaiswal celebrated a major milestone by attending the exciting France vs. Sweden FIFA match in New York, wearing an Argentina jersey, in honor of Lionel Messi. This appearance arrives as he takes a breather following an unusual absence from the most recent ODI selections, with eyes set on rejoining the Test series against New Zealand.

title: Why RBI’s new NBFC rules may force Tata Sons into the market
description: The Reserve Bank of India's updated master directions have effectively thwarted Tata Sons' bid to escape mandatory listing. New rules, particularly on indirect public funds and overseas investments, block the holding company's transition to an unregulated core investment entity. This move tightens RBI's oversight, focusing on economic substance over legal form, leaving Tata Sons with limited options.

title: Congress sought Savarkar’s release in 1923, grandnephew tells Pune court
Stebin Ben defends Kriti Sanon amid marriage buzz: ‘Why rush into something so important?’
How submarines launch ballistic missiles without blowing themselves up
description: Submarine-launched ballistic missiles utilize an innovative 'cold launch' method, setting a new global standard for safety. This technique propels missiles via high-pressure gas, preventing the ignition of rocket engines inside the submarine. Originating from Cold War developments, it enables launches from underwater, significantly bolstering nuclear deterrence and the safety of vessels and crews. Modern SLBMs, like India's K-4, integrate this essential technology.

title: Mumbai rain: Over 200 mm downpour triggers waterlogging, traffic disruptions

description: During 2025-26, the United States remained India's second-largest trading partner, with merchandise exports to the country rising 0.92% year-on-year to $87.3 billion.

title: From ‘Mugshot’ watches to crypto: How Donald Trump made $2.3 billion after returning to the White House
FIFA WC Golden Boot: Kane draws level with Haaland, closes in on Messi & Mbappé
description: Harry Kane's stunning late brace against Congo DR has ignited the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race, propelling him to five goals, just one behind leaders Messi and Mbappé.  Kane's heroics secured England's comeback win and kept their World Cup dreams alive, setting up an enthralling battle for the top scorer title as the knockout stages commence. He also surpassed Pelé on the all-time World Cup scoring list.

description: An old video of Siya Goyal, accused in her fiancé Ketan Agarwal's murder, has resurfaced, showing her in a nightclub using abusive language. Another clip shows her at a family function weeks before the alleged crime. Investigators are now scrutinizing Goyal's handling of Agarwal's mobile phone post-death, suspecting evidence tampering. The couple allegedly planned the murder, with a crime scene recreation underway.

Has obtaining cop cover become 8th ritual for a valid marriage: HC
description: Madhya Pradesh High Court has cautioned lawyers against advising newlyweds to seek police protection without genuine threats, calling it a potential "eighth ritual" after marriage. The court's observation came during a hearing where a woman alleged an advocate charged Rs 40,000 for her marriage and promised police protection. The bench emphasized advocates shouldn't exploit young couples and directed an investigation into the institution arranging marriages.

Mahua names 16 'BJP goons' over mob attack, alleges police watched as eggs hurled
description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra alleged a mob attack by BJP workers at a party office in West Bengal's Nadia district. She identified 16 individuals, claiming police remained silent spectators for four hours as protesters hurled eggs and mud. Moitra was later escorted out by police. The BJP denied involvement, suggesting internal TMC conflict, while other opposition leaders condemned the incident and police inaction.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' clears censor: Vijay's final film likely to hit theatres this month
description: Vijay's final film, 'Jana Nayagan,' has reportedly cleared its censor hurdle, receiving an 'A' certificate. This significant development resolves the major issue that delayed its release. Fans are eagerly anticipating an official announcement for the theatrical debut, with expectations high for the film's arrival later this month. Directed by H Vinoth, it features a star-studded cast and music by Anirudh Ravichander.

title: After losing home in Kashmir, family loses only child in Delhi hit-and-run
description: Parents of Sarthak Mattoo, killed in a Delhi hit-and-run, have released an emotional video alleging police inaction and questioning the investigation. Their only child was struck by a Mahindra Thar on June 25, with the SUV allegedly fleeing the scene. The family highlights concerns over delayed blood sample collection, potentially hindering alcohol detection, and pleads with top leaders for justice.

title: Why IRDAI has brought in a statutory body to curb dark patterns in insurance

Hilarious! Djokovic pranks ball girl, leaves Wimbledon crowd in splits - Watch
description: Novak Djokovic entertained Wimbledon fans with a playful prank on a ball girl during his second-round match against Stefanos Tsitsipas before cruising to a 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 victory. The Serbian later apologised jokingly for the prank and said it helped him relax. Djokovic's win kept him on course for a record-equalling eighth Wimbledon title and a 25th Grand Slam crown.

2 days after giving birth, she travelled 200 km for exam, became TN's 1st tribal judge
description: V. Sripathy, a 23-year-old from a remote Tamil Nadu village, achieved a historic feat by clearing the civil judge examination just two days after giving birth. Battling limited infrastructure and societal expectations, her journey from a farmer's daughter to the first civil judge from her tribal community exemplifies extraordinary grit and the power of education. Her determination inspires countless young women, proving that dreams can overcome significant obstacles.

FIFA WC: Ronaldo shares a 5-word message for Portugal fans ahead of R32 clash
description: Cristiano Ronaldo urged Portugal fans to "make us feel at home" ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against Croatia. The match coincides with the first death anniversary of former Portugal striker Diogo Jota. Ronaldo, who recently became Portugal's all-time leading World Cup scorer, will aim to lead his side past Luka Modric and Croatia.

'Happy Birthday': Ahead of July 4, Trump says he delivered his promise on 'plummeting' oil prices
description: Former President Donald Trump announced that the Freedom Fuel Network will slash gas prices at 25 Philadelphia-area stations on July 3rd as a "Happy Birthday" gift ahead of America's 250th anniversary. He highlighted falling oil prices, urging other retailers to follow suit and linking the move to his economic agenda. Trump asserted that gas prices would soon reach pre-Iran excursion lows.

Tamil Nadu govt moves SC against Madras HC order banning cow slaughter
description: Tamil Nadu has approached the Supreme Court challenging the Madras High Court's sweeping ban on cow and calf slaughter. The state argues the High Court's order, based on a 1976 ruling, contradicts the Tamil Nadu Animal Preservation Act, 1958. This Act permits the slaughter of older, unfit cows under specific conditions, a provision the state contends was ignored by the blanket prohibition.

100+ prominent India, Pakistan voices appeal for 'confidence building measures'
description: Over 100 prominent Indian and Pakistani citizens have penned an open letter to Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Shehbaz Sharif, urging them to restore peace and dialogue. The signatories propose confidence-building measures like reinstating full diplomatic ties and resuming normal visa services. They emphasize that continued hostility hinders the prosperity and secure future of millions of young people in both nations, stressing the need for cooperation over confrontation.

Ram temple donation row: How counting room key became central to theft probe
description: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into the Ram Temple donation theft, planning to question accused Ramshankar Yadav, alias Tinnu, in jail. Fresh disclosures suggest Tinnu had a key to the donation counting room, potentially enabling him to manipulate cash offerings. Investigators are also examining claims of unrestricted access to administrative areas due to proximity to a former trustee, and how accused coordinated movements to evade CCTV surveillance during counting.

Sarpanches in Rajasthan, MP linked to trafficking of children
description: An organized child trafficking network has been uncovered in Ahmedabad, with children being recruited from impoverished families in Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. Village chiefs allegedly act as brokers, facilitating the movement of children for begging at lucrative spots like temples and traffic signals. Authorities have identified 276 trafficked children and are preparing criminal cases against exploiters, aiming to rehabilitate the victims.

Bribes given for jobs in Ram temple? Cops probe role of trust member
description: Ayodhya police are investigating claims of bribery for appointments at the Ram temple. An arrested individual's statements have pointed towards a Trust member's alleged involvement in a recruitment racket, where nearly 125 employees may have paid for their positions. Investigators are scrutinizing financial records and properties linked to the Trust member, while also examining the roles of two other arrested individuals, who are reportedly relatives of the Trust member.

description: Sociology students are voicing their discontent with the latest UGC-NET exam, pointing out critical spelling mistakes, incorrect names of well-known theorists, and awkward translations in Hindi. Errors like confusing 'Ritzer' with 'Putzer' and translating 'social' as 'oval' have raised red flags. Many candidates suspect that certain questions seemed AI-generated, straying from the established syllabus and hindering their understanding during the exam.

Fresh violence in Manipur as 20 houses in 3 villages set ablaze
description: Fresh violence erupted in Manipur's Kamjong district as unidentified attackers torched over 20 houses across three villages. Phaimol, a Kuki village, was set ablaze, followed by suspected retaliatory attacks on Tangkhul Naga villages. While security forces intervened, several structures were damaged. Investigations are underway to identify the perpetrators, with a Kuki group condemning the attacks and alleging involvement of armed cadres.

She was 'too fat', incapable of conceiving: Karnataka man strangles wife to death
description: A 29-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her husband in Dharwad over dowry demands and taunts about her weight. Police have arrested the husband and three others in connection with the death of Priyanka Kamalakar. The victim's family reported continuous harassment, with the husband reportedly also humiliating her about her physique. An altercation at their residence allegedly led to the fatal strangulation.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.
